title: Bionano Announces Publication Showing Utility of Optical Genome Mapping in Clinical Research of Infant & Toddler T-ALL
text: Bionano Genomics announced a peer-reviewed publication demonstrating how optical genome mapping (OGM) can detect cancer-causing structural variants in infants and toddlers with T-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (T-ALL). The research, led by Manon Delafoy from Institut Necker Enfants Malades and French pediatric hematology centers, analyzed 27 T-ALL cases in children under 3 years old using targeted sequencing, OGM, and RNA sequencing. One-third of cases (9 of 27) carried NKX2 gene rearrangements previously uncharacterized in this age group. Infants/toddlers showed distinct genetic profiles compared to older children, lacking TLX1/3 dysregulation. Despite aggressive presentation, youngest subjects achieved 75.4% five-year overall survival, matching older pediatric subjects at 75.2%. Specific structural variants (NKX2, KMT2A, STAG2::LMO2) identified a subgroup with 100% survival rates. According to Bionano's president and CEO, the technology provides a more complete view of the genome, revealing structural alterations that would otherwise remain hidden.
